description: Curated public-repository validation runs used to harden RegressProof.

candidateQueue.rationale: Compact TypeScript HTTP client with source and test changes that may produce clear diff-to-check evidence.
candidateQueue.firstPass: Use sparse shallow checkout, inspect recent source/test commits, then pin a small code commit before any lightweight validation.

candidateQueue.rationale: Small TypeScript fetch utility that appears suitable for the public-repo runner model.
candidateQueue.firstPass: Target a recent non-doc source/test commit and avoid dependency-heavy checks until setup cost is known.

candidateQueue.rationale: Focused TypeScript state-management package with tests and type-oriented behavior changes.
candidateQueue.firstPass: Pin a compact source/test diff and capture changed files before attempting install-backed checks.

candidateQueue.rationale: Mature Python CLI framework that can broaden validation beyond TypeScript once a small commit is pinned.
candidateQueue.firstPass: Select a small Python source/test commit and begin with metadata plus path-snapshot evidence before any pytest run.

candidateQueue.rationale: Smaller Python plugin-system library suitable for exercising Python attribution without a large app stack.
candidateQueue.firstPass: Pin a recent code commit with changed tests or source, then decide whether lightweight local checks are feasible.

runs.notes: Pinned reproducible run for OpenAI Codex provider transport normalization changes.
Floating-main runner smoke after OpenClaw advanced to a changelog-only latest commit.
